pages.title: Welcome to Safe Internet & Link Safety
pages.subtitle: Learn how to identify suspicious links and practice safer browsing habits online.

pages.title: Why link safety matters
pages.text:
Many cyberattacks begin when someone clicks a malicious link. A link may lead to:
  • A fake login page designed to steal passwords
  • A website that downloads malware
  • A page that tricks users into sharing sensitive information
Being cautious with links helps prevent many security incidents.

pages.title: Check links before clicking
pages.text: Before clicking a link, take a moment to inspect it. Many email programs and browsers allow you to hover over a link to see the real destination. If the link address looks unfamiliar, misspelled, or unrelated to the sender, it may be unsafe.

image.alt: Hovering over a link to see the destination

pages.title: Shortened links
pages.text:
Shortened links hide the real destination of a website. Examples include:
  • bit.ly
  • tinyurl.com
  • shortened links used on social media
Because the true destination is hidden, shortened links should be treated carefully unless they come from a trusted source.

pages.title: Look-alike domains
pages.text:
Attackers often create websites that look very similar to legitimate ones. Examples include:
  • micr0soft.com instead of microsoft.com
  • paypaI.com using a capital letter instead of lowercase L
Always read website addresses carefully before entering login credentials.

pages.title: HTTPS and secure connections
pages.text: Most legitimate websites use HTTPS, which encrypts communication between your browser and the website. You can often see a lock icon in the browser address bar. HTTPS does not guarantee a website is trustworthy, but it is an important indicator of a secure connection.

pages.title: Pop-ups and downloads
pages.text:
Unexpected pop-ups may try to convince you to download software or click urgent warnings. Examples include:
  • Fake virus alerts
  • Unexpected software downloads
  • Requests to install browser extensions
Only download files or software from trusted sources.

pages.title: Safe browsing habits
pages.text:
To browse the internet more safely:
  • Pause before clicking unfamiliar links
  • Verify website addresses carefully
  • Avoid downloading unknown files
  • Use trusted websites when entering credentials
Taking a few seconds to verify a link can prevent many cyber incidents.

pages.title: Ready for the quiz?
pages.subtitle: This quiz will test your understanding of link safety and safe browsing habits.
